name: Sky
description_short: Kasasa is a financial technology provider that helps credit unions and community banks grow deposits and member engagement through innovative checking accounts, loans, and digital platforms.
description_long:
Kasasa is a financial technology and consulting firm that partners with credit unions and community banks to modernize their deposit and lending products. Founded on the principle of addressing 'phantom growth'—deposits that are expensive and don't retain—Kasasa has spent over 20 years working with more than 850 financial institutions to create sustainable member relationships. The company is not itself a credit union but rather a B2B service provider that enables credit unions to compete more effectively in the market.

Kasasa's core offerings include Kasasa Checking Accounts (designed to grow low-cost core deposits), Take-Back® Loan features (empowering borrowers with payment flexibility), FIRSTBranch® websites (digital banking platforms), and INMO® digital account opening tools. They also provide SureLock™ deposit products and comprehensive marketing and compliance support. The platform is designed to reduce the cost of funds, increase member engagement, and create stickier financial relationships—particularly with younger consumers.

What distinguishes Kasasa is their combination of product innovation with dedicated consulting and marketing support. Rather than selling standalone products, they provide proactive performance analysis, regional consultant partnerships, and tested marketing assets. Their data shows clients achieve 6x higher average balances per account, 45% more non-interest income, and 2/3 less reliance on expensive CDs. This end-to-end approach—product design, go-to-market strategy, compliance guidance, and ongoing optimization—sets them apart from pure software vendors.

Kasasa is genuinely designed to solve real problems for credit unions facing deposit challenges and member retention issues. However, potential clients should understand they are selecting a technology and consulting partner, not a financial institution themselves. The value proposition relies on effective implementation and adoption by member-facing staff. While their track record with 850+ institutions suggests broad applicability, success ultimately depends on how well individual credit unions execute Kasasa's strategies within their specific markets.
services: Kasasa Checking Accounts—demand deposit products designed for low-cost core deposit growth
Take-Back® Loan feature—loan product allowing borrowers to pay ahead and access extra dollars flexibly
FIRSTBranch® websites—full-featured digital banking and account opening platforms
INMO® digital account opening—omnichannel onboarding platform for completing applications in 5 minutes
SureLock™ deposit products—specialized deposit account designs
Marketing strategy and optimization—tested email programs, multi-channel campaigns, and digital library assets
Compliance and product design guidance—expert consultation on regulatory requirements and product structure
Dedicated regional consulting—ongoing performance analysis, training, and success management
Cost of Deposits Calculator—tool to model financial impact of switching products
Data analytics and performance reporting—regular review and optimization of Kasasa program results
Retail activation training—initial and ongoing staff training for member-facing implementation
Account acquisition and conversion optimization—strategies to increase digital traffic and member engagement
pros: Kasasa Checking Accounts reportedly deliver 6x higher average balances per account compared to traditional products
Generates 45% more non-interest income for partner institutions while reducing reliance on expensive CDs by 2/3
FIRSTBranch® websites reduce account acquisition costs by 56% and serve 850+ financial institutions
INMO® digital account opening increases completion rates by 26% and enables full account funding in 5 minutes
Dedicated regional consultants provide ongoing performance analysis, training, and proactive optimization guidance
Take-Back® Loan feature creates borrowers 2X more likely to open checking accounts, deepening member relationships
Over 20 years of data and proven marketing assets from their digital library guide strategy and reduce guesswork
cons: Kasasa is a B2B vendor, not a direct consumer financial institution—credit unions must partner with and implement their platform
Success depends heavily on effective retail activation and staff training; poor execution by credit unions could limit results
Pricing and contract terms are not disclosed on the website, making cost-benefit analysis difficult for prospective institutions
The website does not provide detailed compliance or regulatory information, which is critical for credit union decision-making
Client success stories on the website are incomplete and lack detailed performance metrics or independent verification
best_for: Credit unions seeking to reduce deposit costs and compete for younger, more engaged members
Community banks and credit unions struggling with expensive CD reliance and member retention
Financial institutions wanting an end-to-end technology and consulting partner, not just a software vendor
Credit unions looking to modernize their digital presence and account opening experience
diagnosis: Kasasa is essential for credit unions competing in deposit markets and seeking to reduce member acquisition costs while building stickier relationships. The main caveat is that Kasasa itself is not a credit union or direct financial provider—it is a technology and consulting platform that credit unions must adopt, implement, and actively use to see results; poor execution or insufficient staff buy-in will limit effectiveness.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Sky — Credit Union in Livingston, MT | CreditDoc
meta_description: Sky — NCUA-insured credit union in Livingston, MT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
